name: New Mexico-Style Barbecue Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, melt the butter.
2. Add the onion and garlic; sweat until translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in the diced tomatoes and cook for an additional 15 minutes.
4. Mix in the remaining ingredients (ketchup, Dijon mustard, whipped cream, honey, cayenne, Pasilla chile powder, Worcestershire sauce).
5. Cook for another 20 minutes until thickened.
6. Puree the mixture using a blender or food processor.
7. Pour into a bowl and allow to cool at room temperature.
8. The sauce will keep in the refrigerator for up to one week or frozen for several months.
